'''隠津島神社（おきつしまじんじゃ）'''は[[福島県]][[郡山市]]湖南町にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式]]内社（[[式内社]]）。[[平安時代]]に[[筑紫国]][[宇佐郡]]の[[宗像大社]]より勧請し、隠津島神社と号したのが始まりだという。社叢（境内の森林）は原生林に覆われ、福島県指定[[天然記念物]]、[[福島県緑の百景]]にも選定されている。
== 関連項目 == *[[隠津島神社(福島・木幡)]] *[[神社一覧]]
[[category:式内小社|おきつしまじんじゃ]] [[category:福島県の神社|おきつしまじんじゃ]]

月次祭（毎月24日）| 本殿=入母屋造}} '''隠津島神社（おきつしまじんじゃ）'''は[[福島県]][[郡山市]]湖南町にある[[神社]]。[[延喜式]]内社（[[式内社]]）、陸奥國安積郡三座の一座である。
[[平安時代]]に[[筑紫国]][[宇佐郡]]の[[宗像大社]]より勧請し、隠津島神社と号したのが始まりだという。
社叢（境内の森林）は原生林に覆われ神秘的な雰囲気があり、福島県指定[[天然記念物]]、[[福島県緑の百景]]にも選定されている。
境内及びその周辺には、御神岩たる烏帽子岩、別宮たる風穴堂（蛇神を祀る）、往古より禊の場とされた菅滝などがある。 == 関連項目 == *[[木幡山#隠津島神社|隠津島神社 (二本松市)]] *[[神社一覧]] {{DEFAULTSORT:おきつしましんしや}} {{Pref-stub|pref=福島県}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:郡山市]]

境内及びその周辺には、御神岩たる烏帽子岩、別宮たる風穴堂（蛇神を祀る）、往古より禊の場とされた菅滝などがある。 == 関連項目 == *[[木幡山#隠津島神社|隠津島神社 (二本松市)]] *[[神社一覧]] {{DEFAULTSORT:おきつしましんしや}} {{Pref-stub|pref=福島県}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:郷社]] [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:福島県の観光地]] [[Category:郡山市]] [[Category:日本の森林]]

多岐都比売命 |社格 = 式内小社論社・旧郷社 |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = 流造 |別名 = お菅様 |札所等 = |例祭 = 7月23日～25日 |神事 = }} '''隠津島神社'''（おきつしまじんじゃ）は[[福島県]][[郡山市]]湖南町、[[福島県道235号羽鳥福良線]]沿いに鎮座する[[神社]]。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』[[陸奥国]][[安積郡]]に載る「隠津島神社」に比定される[[論社|式内社論社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 地元では、「お菅様（おすげさま）」と呼ばれている。 == 祭神 == [[市杵島比売命]]、[[多紀理毘売命|多岐理毘売命]]、[[多岐都比売命]]を祀る。 == 由緒 == [[平安時代]]に[[筑紫国]][[宇佐郡]]{{要検証|date=2011年9月|title=筑前国宗像郡では？}}の[[宗像大社]]より[[勧請]]し、隠津島神社と号したのが始まりだという。社叢（境内の森林）は約15[[ヘクタール]]にもおよぶ鬱蒼とした原生林に覆われ、まさに神秘的な雰囲気を有する。福島県指定[[天然記念物]]、[[福島県緑の百景]]にも選定されている。 境内及びその周辺には、御神岩である烏帽子岩、別宮である風穴堂（蛇神を祀る）、往古より[[禊]]の場とされた菅滝などがある。 福島県道235号羽鳥福良線の冬季閉鎖期間中（例年、12月中旬から4月中旬頃まで）は、交通手段がないため参拝はできない。 == 関連項目 == *[[隠津島神社 (二本松市)]] {{Pref-stub|pref=福島県}} {{デフォルトソート:おきつしましんしや}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:郡山市]] [[Category:福島県の観光地]] [[Category:日本の森林]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: 筑紫国＝筑前国＋筑後国となるため間違いではない。神社の由緒にも筑紫の国との記載有り
revision.text:

多岐都比売命 |社格 = 式内小社論社・旧郷社 |創建 = 不明 |本殿 = 流造 |別名 = お杉様 お菅様 |札所等 = |例祭 = 7月23日 - 25日 |神事 = }} '''隠津島神社'''（おきつしまじんじゃ）は[[福島県]][[郡山市]]湖南町、[[福島県道235号羽鳥福良線]]沿いに鎮座する[[神社]]。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』[[陸奥国]][[安積郡]]に載る「隠津島神社」に比定される[[論社|式内社論社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 地元では、「お杉様（おすぎさま）お菅様（おすげさま）」と呼ばれている。 == 祭神 == [[市杵島比売命]]、[[多紀理毘売命|多岐理毘売命]]、[[多岐都比売命]]を祀る。 == 由緒 == 社伝によれば、[[平安時代]]に[[筑紫国]][[宇佐郡]]の[[宗像大社]]より[[勧請]]し、隠津島神社と号したのが始まりだという。社叢（境内の森林）は約15[[ヘクタール]]にもおよぶ鬱蒼とした原生林に覆われ、まさに神秘的な雰囲気を有する。福島県指定[[天然記念物]]、[[福島県緑の百景]]にも選定されている。 境内及びその周辺には、御神岩である烏帽子岩、別宮である風穴堂（蛇神を祀る）、往古より[[禊]]の場とされた菅滝などがある。 == アクセス == 福島県道235号羽鳥福良線の冬季閉鎖期間中（例年、12月中旬から4月中旬頃まで）は、交通手段がないため参拝はできない。 郡山駅から車利用で約1時間。付近に路線バス等の運行はない。（最寄りバス停は会津バスバス停で約7km） == 関連項目 == *[[隠津島神社 (二本松市)]] *[[隠津島神社 (郡山市喜久田町堀之内)]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:おきつしましんしや}} [[Category:福島県の神社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:福島県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:郡山市の歴史]] [[Category:郡山市の建築物]]

revision.text:
{{Cite sources| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name = Okitsushima Shrine |Image = [[File:隠津島神社（郡山市湖南町）.jpg|250px|Okitsushima Shrine]] |Location = {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi Fukura, 7414 Fukura-yama, Fukushima Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|20|05.4|N|140|04|57.9|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}}
{{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate Names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: - **Eboshiiwa**, a sacred rock - **Kazaanado**, a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity - **Suge-no-Taki**, a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Koriyama, Kikutada)|ja|隠津島神社 (郡山市喜久田町堀之内)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Koriyama]] [[Category:Engishiki shrines in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Former Township Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[ja:隠津島神社 (郡山市湖南町福良)]]

{{Cite sources| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |Name = Okitsushima Shrine |Image = [[File:隠津島神社（郡山市湖南町）.jpg|250px|Okitsushima Shrine]] |Location = {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi Fukura, 7414 Fukura-yama, Fukushima Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|37|20|05.4|N|140|04|57.9|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}}
{{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate Names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. {{Cite sources| date = November 2024}} {{Shrine |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:隠津島神社（郡山市湖南町）.jpg|250px|Okitsushima Shrine]] |location = {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi Fukura, 7414 Fukura-yama, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|20|05.4|N|140|04|57.9|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}}
{{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: - **Eboshiiwa**, a sacred rock - **Kazaanado**, a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity - **Suge-no-Taki**, a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: - **Eboshiiwa**, a sacred rock - **Kazaanado**, a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity - **Suge-no-Taki**, a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{Cite sources| date = November 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:隠津島神社（郡山市湖南町）.jpg|250px|Okitsushima Shrine]] |location = {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi Fukura, 7414 Fukura-yama, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|20|05.4|N|140|04|57.9|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}}
{{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: - **Eboshiiwa**, a sacred rock - **Kazaanado**, a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity - **Suge-no-Taki**, a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{Cite sources| date = November 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:隠津島神社（郡山市湖南町）.jpg|250px|Okitsushima Shrine]] |location = {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi Fukura, 7414 Fukura-yama, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|20|05.4|N|140|04|57.9|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}}
{{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: - **Eboshiiwa**, a sacred rock - **Kazaanado**, a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity - **Suge-no-Taki**, a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{Cite sources| date = November 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:隠津島神社（郡山市湖南町）.jpg|250px|Okitsushima Shrine]] |location = {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi Fukura, 7414 Fukura-yama, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|20|05.4|N|140|04|57.9|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}}
{{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: - **Eboshiiwa**, a sacred rock - **Kazaanado**, a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity - **Suge-no-Taki**, a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{Cite sources| date = November 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:隠津島神社（郡山市湖南町）.jpg|250px|Okitsushima Shrine]] |location = {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi Fukura, 7414 Fukura-yama, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|20|05.4|N|140|04|57.9|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}
{{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{Cite sources| date = November 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:隠津島神社（郡山市湖南町）.jpg|250px|Okitsushima Shrine]] |location = {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi Fukura, 7414 Fukura-yama, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|20|05.4|N|140|04|57.9|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}
{{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{Cite sources| date = November 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:隠津島神社（郡山市湖南町）.jpg|250px|Okitsushima Shrine]] |location = {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi Fukura, 7414 Fukura-yama, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|20|05.4|N|140|04|57.9|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}
{{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Koriyama, Kikutada)|ja|隠津島神社 (郡山市喜久田町堀之内)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okitsu Shrine revision.text:
{{Cite sources| date = November 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:隠津島神社（郡山市湖南町）.jpg|250px|Okitsushima Shrine]] |location = {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi Fukura, 7414 Fukura-yama, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|20|05.4|N|140|04|57.9|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}
{{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{Cite sources| date = November 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:隠津島神社（郡山市湖南町）.jpg|250px|Okitsushima Shrine]] |location = {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi Fukura, 7414 Fukura-yama, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|20|05.4|N|140|04|57.9|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}
{{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{Cite sources| date = November 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:隠津島神社（郡山市湖南町）.jpg|250px|Okitsushima Shrine]] |location = {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi Fukura, 7414 Fukura-yama, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|20|05.4|N|140|04|57.9|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}
{{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Koriyama, Kikutada)|ja|隠津島神社 (郡山市喜久田町堀之内)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Okitsu Shrine Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. {{Cite sources| date = November 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:隠津島神社（郡山市湖南町）.jpg|250px|Okitsushima Shrine]] |location = {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi Fukura, 7414 Fukura-yama, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|20|05.4|N|140|04|57.9|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}
{{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{Cite sources| date = November 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:隠津島神社（郡山市湖南町）.jpg|250px|Okitsushima Shrine]] |location = {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi Fukura, 7414 Fukura-yama, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|20|05.4|N|140|04|57.9|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}
{{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Koriyama, Kikutada)|ja|隠津島神社 (郡山市喜久田町堀之内)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]][[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:C tier 1]] {{Cite sources| date = November 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:隠津島神社（郡山市湖南町）.jpg|250px|Okitsushima Shrine]] |location = {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi Fukura, 7414 Fukura-yama, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|20|05.4|N|140|04|57.9|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}
{{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国}}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{Cite sources| date = November 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:隠津島神社（郡山市湖南町）.jpg|250px|Okitsushima Shrine]] |location = {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi Fukura, 7414 Fukura-yama, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|20|05.4|N|140|04|57.9|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}
{{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Koriyama, Kikutada)|ja|隠津島神社 (郡山市喜久田町堀之内)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]][[Category:C]][[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:C tier 1]] {{Cite sources| date = November 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:隠津島神社（郡山市湖南町）.jpg|250px|Okitsushima Shrine]] |location = {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi Fukura, 7414 Fukura-yama, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|20|05.4|N|140|04|57.9|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}
{{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{Cite sources| date = November 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:隠津島神社（郡山市湖南町）.jpg|250px|Okitsushima Shrine]] |location = {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi Fukura, 7414 Fukura-yama, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|20|05.4|N|140|04|57.9|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}
{{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Koriyama, Kikutada)|ja|隠津島神社 (郡山市喜久田町堀之内)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:C tier 1]] {{Cite sources| date = November 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:隠津島神社（郡山市湖南町）.jpg|250px|Okitsushima Shrine]] |location = {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi Fukura, 7414 Fukura-yama, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|20|05.4|N|140|04|57.9|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}
{{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away.== See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Koriyama, Kikutada)|ja|隠津島神社 (郡山市喜久田町堀之内)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] {{Cite sources| date = November 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:隠津島神社（郡山市湖南町）.jpg|250px|Okitsushima Shrine]] |location = {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi Fukura, 7414 Fukura-yama, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|20|05.4|N|140|04|57.9|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}
{{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |Alternate names = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{Cite sources| date = November 2024}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Okitsushima Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:隠津島神社（郡山市湖南町）.jpg|250px|Okitsushima Shrine]] |location = {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi Fukura, 7414 Fukura-yama, Fukushima Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|37|20|05.4|N|140|04|57.9|E|region:JP-07_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}}
{{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}}
{{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |alternate_name = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province }}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Koriyama, Kikutada)|ja|隠津島神社 (郡山市喜久田町堀之内)}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:C tier 1]] {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=draft}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |alternate_name = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市|12=draft}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線|12=draft}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province|12=draft}}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社|12=draft}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社|12=draft}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=draft}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請|12=draft}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡|12=draft}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物|12=draft}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景|12=draft}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊|12=draft}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Koriyama, Kikutada)|ja|隠津島神社 (郡山市喜久田町堀之内)|12=draft}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:C tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Koriyama]] [[Category:List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Former Township Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]] [[ja:隠津島神社 (郡山市湖南町福良)]] [[sn:kitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]] [[en:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)}}

Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=draft}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請|lt=Enshrinement|1=Kanjō|2=ja|3=分霊|4=es|5=Bunrei|6=id|7=Kanjō|8=zh|9=分靈|10=fr|11=Kanjō|12=draft}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡|12=draft}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物|12=draft}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景|12=draft}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊|12=draft}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)|12=draft}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Koriyama, Kikutada)|ja|隠津島神社 (郡山市喜久田町堀之内)|12=draft}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]] [[Category:completion tier 5]] [[Category:C tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Koriyama]] [[Category:List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Former Township Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]] [[ja:隠津島神社 (郡山市湖南町福良)]] [[sn:kitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]] [[en:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)}}

Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=draft|12=draft}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft|12=draft}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請|lt=Enshrinement|1=Kanjō|2=ja|3=分霊|4=es|5=Bunrei|6=id|7=Kanjō|8=zh|9=分靈|10=fr|11=Kanjō|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡|12=draft|12=draft}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物|12=draft|12=draft}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景|12=draft|12=draft}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊|12=draft|12=draft}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Koriyama, Kikutada)|ja|隠津島神社 (郡山市喜久田町堀之内)|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:C tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shrines in Koriyama]] [[Category:List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Former Township Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]] [[ja:隠津島神社 (郡山市湖南町福良)]] [[sn:kitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]] [[en:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)}}

{{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} |Rank = Minor Shrine of the Engishiki, Former Township Shrine |Foundation = Unknown |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri |alternate_name = Osugi-sama, Osuge-sama |Main Festival = July 23–25 |Rituals = |Map = Japan Fukushima }} '''Okitsushima Shrine''' (隠津島神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Koriyama, Fukushima|ja|郡山市}}, Konan-machi, along {{ill|Fukushima Prefectural Route 235|ja|福島県道235号羽鳥福良線}}. It is identified as one of the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}} shrines in {{ill|Mutsu Province|ja|Mutsu Province}}, Asaka District, and recognized as a {{ill|ronsha|ja|論社}} (shrine of uncertain status). Its former shrine Ranking was {{ill|Gosha|ja|郷社}}. Locally, it is affectionately called "Osugi-sama" or "Osuge-sama." == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島比売命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagirihime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐理毘売命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime-no-Mikoto|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime}} == History == According to tradition, the shrine was founded during the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} through a {{ill|kanjo|ja|勧請|lt=Enshrinement|1=Kanjō|2=ja|3=分霊|4=es|5=Bunrei|6=id|7=Kanjō|8=zh|9=分靈|10=fr|11=Kanjō}} from {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} in {{ill|Usa District, Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国宇佐郡}}. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. Its sacred grove, spanning approximately 15 hectares, is a dense, primeval forest exuding a mysterious and spiritual ambiance. The area has been designated a {{ill|Natural Monument|ja|天然記念物|12=draft|12=draft}} of Fukushima Prefecture and selected as one of the {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture's Top 100 Greenery|ja|福島県緑の百景|12=draft|12=draft}}. The precincts and nearby areas feature sacred sites such as: * '''Eboshiiwa''', a sacred rock * '''Kazaanado''', a subsidiary shrine dedicated to a serpent deity * '''Suge-no-Taki''', a waterfall historically used for purification rituals ({{ill|misogi|ja|禊|12=draft|12=draft}}) == Access == The shrine is inaccessible during the winter closure of Fukushima Prefectural Route 235, typically from mid-December to mid-April. - Approx. 1 hour by car from Koriyama Station. - No local bus service is available. The nearest bus stop (Aizu Bus) is approximately 7 km away. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Okitsushima-jinja (Konan-machi, Koriyama)}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Nihonmatsu)|ja|隠津島神社 (二本松市)|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Okitsushima Shrine (Koriyama, Kikutada)|ja|隠津島神社 (郡山市喜久田町堀之内)|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|隠津島神社 (郡山市湖南町福良)|version=102484926|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:C tier 1]] [[Category:Shrines in Koriyama]] [[Category:List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Former Township Shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Monuments in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] }} [[sn:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]] [[ja:隠津島神社 (郡山市湖南町福良)]] [[sn:kitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]] [[en:Okitsu Shrine (Kōriyama)]]
